A kind, generous, and loyal club president… I feel like there are very few left like you in the modern game.
There was Roman Abramovich, whose approach and decisions turned Chelsea into one of the best in Europe.
Congratulations to you—Paris Saint-Germain now have one of the best club presidents in Europe. 🔥🔥🔥

comments.author: @robertmurphy6772
comments.text: The loss to Ghana in the knockout round following this game was a real disappointment. While the US faced a great Ghanian team, it was a team they could have beaten, and the game could well have gone either way. Had the US beaten Ghana, they would have faced Uruguay, a match where they would have been underdogs but also a match they could win. Hence, the USMNT had a legitimate shot at making the semifinals at the 2010 Cup. (Ghana went on to get robbed by Uruguay after Luis Suarez, showing the class we have all come expect from him, fisted a ball of the goal line; Ghana missed the ensuing penalty kick then lost on penalties, but that's another story.)

"Congrats Arsenal! You made it!

Although I am an Arsenal fan and happy for our achievement, it pains me that you are here attending such a festive event at Addis Ababa Stadium. Have you not heard of the massacre of Orthodox Christians in my country?

More than 53+ of them have been killed, over 1280+ displaced, their homes burnet down, a 101-year-old historic church reduced to ashes, and several others looted in the Arsi, Oromia region recently. We are mourning our loved ones!

I do not think you should turn a deaf ear to our cry. Attending such a concert at this time is a shame to a group like you. It is a disgrace as well to Arsenal."

comments.author: @Masangtea_Pautu
comments.text:
The 2002 FIFA World Cup made me admire Oliver Kahn. Germany were not strong enough compared to some of their other tournament squads, but their captain and goalkeeper conceded only one goal until the final. Germany also lost their main player, Michael Ballack, because of a frustrating yellow card suspension.

Brazil were a little lucky, but they also had the best squad in the tournament — Cafu, Edmílson, Júnior, Lúcio, Carlos, Gilberto Silva, Ronaldinho, Rivaldo, and Ronaldo. Still, Kahn did everything he could.

He was a true leader — like an army commander carrying his squad into battle, always ready for war. For me, he will always be the greatest goalkeeper on the planet. Yes, David de Gea, Iker Casillas, Gianluigi Buffon, and Manuel Neuer were all amazing, but none of them had Oliver Kahn’s personality and leadership presence.
